title: Move Alias Management into the Sidebar Editor
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to manage metric aliases directly in the sidebar panel alongside other metric details, so that I can view and edit all metric information from a single location without switching contexts.
description:
Relocates the alias management interface into the sidebar detail panel, consolidating all metric editing into one place. The sidebar displays aliases in the same read/edit mode pattern used for taxonomies.

In read-only mode, aliases are listed as static text with the primary alias highlighted. In edit mode (triggered by clicking the + icon or the edit button), the alias management form appears with options to add, edit, delete, and mark aliases as primary. Switching back to output mode reflects any alias changes.
acceptance_criteria: Aliases section appears in the sidebar detail panel in read-only mode
Primary alias is visually distinguished in the read-only alias list
Clicking the + icon or edit button opens the alias editor in edit mode
User can add, edit, delete, and mark aliases as primary from the sidebar
Returning to output mode reflects alias changes
Main data table updates when the primary alias is changed
ux_changes: Alias management moves from a separate interface into the sidebar detail panel. Read mode shows a static alias list; edit mode shows the full alias management form with add/edit/delete/primary actions.
implementation_notes: Reuses existing alias CRUD logic from MBT-142, relocated into the sidebar panel component. Implemented in approximately 1.75 hours.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the alias section in the sidebar with read/output mode toggle and submitted screenshots showing both modes. Approved for staging.
